name: Daily Activity Digest
description: Single morning digest in 3 steps. Step 1 (stage, exec): gather the day's cross-agent activity with NO inline LLM and write a staging file. Step 2 (summarize, agent spawn): read the staging file and build a presentation + markdown that conforms to presentation.schema.json, returned via tasks.handoff (NOT by writing the staging file ‚Äî that needs an interpreter and trips OpenClaw's exec-approval gate). Step 3 (publish, exec): read the handoff from brain.db, VALIDATE it against the contract, and post to Discord ‚Äî failing OPEN to a deterministic regex render if the handoff is missing or violates the contract. Parameterized by {{wiki_root}} + {{python_path}} so a fresh install lands the same workflow against any user's wiki + venv. The summarize<->publish seam is pinned by an explicit versioned schema so a producer drift (e.g. block key `content` vs `text`) is caught loudly instead of silently dropping the digest.

variables.name: wiki_root
variables.description: Absolute path to the digital-me wiki root. The installer supplies this from --wiki-root or $DIGITAL_ME_WIKI_ROOT.

variables.name: python_path
variables.description: Absolute path to a python3 interpreter that has the digest package installed. The installer auto-supplies the venv interpreter.

variables.name: staging_path
variables.description: Absolute path to the staging file the stage step writes and the summarize + publish steps read. Default lives in /tmp so it doesn't pollute the wiki tree.

variables.name: summarize_agent_id
variables.description: agentId for the summarize step. Should be an agent that can read a file and call tasks.handoff. Defaults to claude-code-cli (the local-CLI exec worker).

steps.name: Gather raw data and stage for the summarizer
steps.promptTemplate: Run digest.daily_digest --stage with the staging path from this dispatch's command. Gathers the day's cross-agent activity (counts, wiki entries, taste entries, raw prompts) with NO inline LLM and writes the staging JSON.

steps.name: Summarizer builds the presentation from staged data
steps.promptTemplate:
You are building the daily activity digest from raw data staged at `{{staging_path}}`.

## TOOL POLICY (critical ‚Äî read first)
- READ the staging file at `{{staging_path}}` with your Read/file tool, then parse the JSON yourself.
- Do NOT use Bash/cat or any interpreter (node -e / python3 -c / jq / awk) ‚Äî they are approval-gated and will hang unattended. Do NOT write or edit any file. You return your result ONLY via `tasks.handoff` (see Output).

Staging file shape:
```
{
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "tldr": {active_agents, wiki_new, wiki_updated, taste_total, skill_new, skill_merged, skill_evidence, skill_promoted, skill_neither},
  "wiki_entries": [{title, domains, status: "created"|"updated"|"touched"}, ...],
  "taste_entries": [{title, domains, status, fingerprint}, ...],
  "agent_raw_prompts": { "<agent label>": [str, ...] }
}
```

## Your job
Read and understand the day's activity, then build a `presentation` with these blocks IN ORDER:
1. TL;DR text block: a code-fenced aligned table `Agents  Wiki+  Wiki~  Taste` with the counts from `tldr`. If taste_total>0, add a one-line breakdown.
2. `{"type":"divider"}`
3. Wiki: a `header` block `Wiki`, then a `text` block listing each wiki entry as `N. [created | updated | touched] **title** ‚Äî _domains_`.
4. `{"type":"divider"}`
5. Taste: a `header` block `Taste`, then a `text` block listing each taste entry the same way (fingerprint as an italic second line).
6. `{"type":"divider"}`
7. Executive Summary: a `header` block `Executive Summary`, then ONE `text` block ‚Äî a concise 3‚Äì6 sentence narrative of what got accomplished across ALL agents. Synthesize; lead with the single most important thing. Plain editorial prose, no per-agent bullets, strip code paths/IDs. If quiet, say so in one sentence.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT (presentation.schema.json ‚Äî conform EXACTLY)
The publisher VALIDATES your output against this schema and will reject a violation (then fall back to a plain render). Match it exactly:
```json
{
  "title": "Daily Digest ‚Äî <date>",          // non-empty string; a leading emoji is fine
  "tone": "success | info | warning | danger", // success if any wiki/taste growth; info if quiet; warning if breakage
  "blocks": [
    {"type": "text", "text": "<string>"},      // text blocks: key MUST be "text" (NOT "content"), non-empty
    {"type": "header", "text": "<string>"},    // header blocks: key MUST be "text", non-empty
    {"type": "divider"}                          // dividers carry no text
  ]
}
```
CRITICAL: every text/header block's visible string goes under the key `text` (a plain string), never `content`. `type` is one of text|header|divider only.

Also write a parallel plain-markdown version of the same content.

## How you return data (the ONLY way)
Call `tasks.handoff` with:
- `deliverableState`: "complete"
- `summary`: a single fenced ```json block whose object is EXACTLY {"presentation": {title, tone, blocks:[...]}, "markdown": "<full markdown>"}. Nothing else is parsed.
- `recommendedNextStep`: "publish digest"
The publish step reads this handoff back from the orchestrator DB. If you write the staging file instead of handing off, publish will fall back to a plain render and your work is lost.

steps.name: Validate the handoff and publish the digest to Discord
steps.promptTemplate: Run digest.daily_digest --publish with the staging path from this dispatch's command. Reads the summarizer's handoff from brain.db, validates it against presentation.schema.json, and posts to Discord + writes the markdown log. Fails OPEN: if the handoff is missing or violates the contract, it renders deterministically from the staged data (no inline LLM) so the digest still ships.
